goldpy

goldpy.models

Pydantic models used throughout goldpy.

These models draw a line between what the upstream API sends and what the rest of the application wants to work with. The raw models stay close to the Swissquote payload, while the normalized model is shaped for CLI output.

class goldpy.models.QuoteTopo(*, platform, server)

Bases: BaseModel

Describe where a quote came from.

Variables:

  • platform – Trading platform name reported by Swissquote.

  • server – Specific server identifier for that platform.

Parameters:

  • platform (str)

  • server (str)

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class goldpy.models.SpreadProfilePrice(*, spreadProfile, bidSpread, askSpread, bid, ask)

Bases: BaseModel

Represent one spread-profile price entry from the upstream payload.

Variables:

  • spread_profile – Spread profile name, such as prime.

  • bid_spread – Spread component applied on the bid side.

  • ask_spread – Spread component applied on the ask side.

  • bid – Bid price for this profile.

  • ask – Ask price for this profile.

Parameters:

  • spreadProfile (str)

  • bidSpread (Decimal)

  • askSpread (Decimal)

  • bid (Decimal)

  • ask (Decimal)

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {'populate_by_name': True, 'validate_by_alias': True, 'validate_by_name': True}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

property mid: Decimal

Return the midpoint between bid and ask.

property spread: Decimal

Return the raw spread for the quote.

class goldpy.models.RawQuote(*, topo, spreadProfilePrices, ts)

Bases: BaseModel

Represent one raw quote source from Swissquote.

Variables:

  • topo – Metadata about the platform and server that produced the quote.

  • spread_profile_prices – Available prices for each spread profile exposed by the source.

  • ts – Source timestamp in Unix milliseconds.

Parameters:

  • topo (QuoteTopo)

  • spreadProfilePrices (list[SpreadProfilePrice])

  • ts (int)

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {'populate_by_name': True, 'validate_by_alias': True, 'validate_by_name': True}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

property timestamp: datetime

Return ts converted to a timezone-aware UTC datetime.

class goldpy.models.PriceSelection(*, platform, server, spread_profile, value)

Bases: BaseModel

Describe how one side of the displayed quote was chosen.

Variables:

  • platform – Platform that supplied the selected value.

  • server – Server that supplied the selected value.

  • spread_profile – Spread profile attached to the selected value.

  • value – The chosen numeric price.

Parameters:

  • platform (str)

  • server (str)

  • spread_profile (str)

  • value (Decimal)

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class goldpy.models.NormalizedQuote(*, pair, bid, ask, mid, timestamp, selection_mode, sources_considered, bid_selection, ask_selection)

Bases: BaseModel

Represent the quote shape returned by the service layer.

This is the object the CLI prints. It keeps the numbers together with just enough provenance to explain where the bid and ask came from.

Variables:

  • pair – Normalized instrument pair.

  • bid – Selected bid price.

  • ask – Selected ask price.

  • mid – Midpoint shown to the user.

  • timestamp – Timestamp used for the final result.

  • selection_mode – Strategy name used to select the quote.

  • sources_considered – Number of candidate prices considered after filtering.

  • bid_selection – Provenance for the chosen bid.

  • ask_selection – Provenance for the chosen ask.

Parameters:

  • pair (str)

  • bid (Decimal)

  • ask (Decimal)

  • mid (Decimal)

  • timestamp (datetime)

  • selection_mode (str)

  • sources_considered (int)

  • bid_selection (PriceSelection)

  • ask_selection (PriceSelection)

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

serialize_decimal_fields(value)

Serialize decimal price fields as strings for stable JSON output.

Parameters:

value (Decimal)

Return type:

str
